disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Gradual onset of chest tightness and expiratory wheeze from the evening, worsening over hours
trigger_question_examples: When did the breathing trouble start?
How has it changed through the night?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It came on around 8 tonight — tight chest and a wheeze — and it's just kept getting worse over the last few hours.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The reliever inhaler is giving little or no benefit tonight
trigger_question_examples: What happens when you use your inhaler?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: That's the scary part — I've used my blue puffer and it just isn't doing anything this time.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Cannot lie flat — orthopnoea with coughing spasms when supine, relieved by sitting upright
trigger_question_examples: Can you lie down flat, or does it make the breathing worse?
Is it worse in any particular position?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I can't lie flat at all — the moment I do I start coughing and feel like I'm choking, so I'm sitting up in a chair.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Adult-onset asthma with adult-onset allergic rhinitis and recurrent nasal polyps
trigger_question_examples: When did your asthma and hay fever start — as a child or as an adult?
Do you get nasal polyps or a persistently blocked nose?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: My asthma and hay fever only started in adulthood, and I keep getting nasal polyps — my nose is blocked most of the time.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Patient recalls a recent routine blood test that reportedly showed a raised eosinophil count
trigger_question_examples: Have you had any recent blood tests?
Did your GP mention anything unusual on recent bloods?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Now that you mention it, my GP said a recent blood test showed a high count of one of the white cells — an eosinophil count, I think they called it.
disclosure_items.patient_deflection_template: I don't really remember the details of the blood test, sorry.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No fever, no coloured or purulent sputum, no pleuritic or exertional chest pain
trigger_question_examples: Any fever or coughing up coloured phlegm?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No fever, I'm not bringing up any coloured phlegm, and there's no sharp chest pain — just the tightness and the wheeze.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Can currently still speak in full sentences; lips not blue; feeling very anxious and panicky
trigger_question_examples: Can you speak in full sentences, or only a few words at a time?
Do your lips or fingertips look blue?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I can still get a full sentence out, and my lips don't look blue, but I'm panicking and it's getting harder.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Lives alone with no one present to help during the acute episode
trigger_question_examples: Is anyone there with you right now?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, I live on my own and there's nobody here with me tonight.
